title: Review-rules scan artifact
description: Output of the review-rules layer for one review: which rules applied to the diff and any violations found. Written to plans/<plan>/artifacts/rule-scan.json by code-review / review-pr. NOT a required gate artifact (never listed in stage-policy requires:) — its presence is an AI-applied boundary the gate does not force. When PRESENT, the gate validates these fields (incl. the severity + verdict enums) and refuses a contradiction: if any violation is critical, this artifact's own verdict must be BLOCKED and a present review-decision must not be PASS or PASS_WITH_RISK.

rules_applied.description: Stable ids of the rules that applied to this diff (rule_view.build_rule_scan → rules_applied). The audit trail for what the reviewer was asked to check.

violations.description: One entry per rule violation found in the diff. Empty = clean scan.

severity.description: critical → blocks (review verdict must be BLOCKED); info → advisory.

file.description: Path of the offending changed file.

line.description: Line of the violation, or null if file-level.

finding.description: What was found and why it violates the rule.

directive.description: Optional: the specific ALWAYS/NEVER directive within the rule body that was violated (rule_id is file-level; a rule body holds many directives, so this pins the directive-level audit).

verdict.description: Scan verdict. A critical violation must yield BLOCKED; info-only may be PASS_WITH_RISK; clean is PASS.

reviewer.description: Actor that produced the scan (attribution, e.g. user:alice).

changed_files.description: The exact set of files the producer reviewed (working-tree or PR-range the reviewer scoped). This is the SINGLE diff source the coverage-gate reads — it never re-derives git, so the universe it checks is exactly what was reviewed (no lost/extra-file skew).
